title: totopo.yaml
description: Configuration file for totopo — secure AI dev containers

workspace_id.description: Unique workspace identifier. Used for container naming and cache directory. Lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens only.

env_file.description: Path to env file relative to totopo.yaml (e.g. '.env'). Injected into container at runtime via --env-file.

shadow_paths.description: Gitignore-style patterns. Matching paths are hidden from the agent — the container gets an empty, isolated copy instead. Patterns without a '/' match at any depth (e.g. 'node_modules' matches all nested node_modules too). Patterns with a '/' match relative to the workspace root. Supports globs: * (any chars), ? (single char), {a,b} (alternatives).

profiles.description: Named Dockerfile profiles. Each profile defines a dockerfile_hook with raw Dockerfile instructions appended to the base image.

description.description: Human-readable description of this profile shown as a hint during profile selection.

dockerfile_hook.description: Raw Dockerfile instructions appended after the base image layers. Use for installing additional runtimes, tools, or dependencies.

ports.description: Host ports published into the container, identity-mapped and loopback-only (127.0.0.1:N:N), so a web tool inside the container is reachable at http://localhost:<port> on the host. Each resolved port is remembered per workspace (sticky) across container recreation.

port.description: Desired host port and the scan start. Must be 1024-65535 (the CLI runs unprivileged).

ifTaken.description: On collision: 'fail' (default) refuses to start; 'next' scans upward from port for a free host port.

env.description: Env var injected into the container with the RESOLVED port number. Required when ifTaken is 'next'.
